scope: AAAI 2027 main-paper sync from the figure-driven ACL candidate story

local_visual_review.notes: The AAAI PDF uses the official two-column letter layout and anonymous submission metadata. The first three rendered pages have no large red-material fallback artifact. Figure 1 is pushed to page 2 by the AAAI style, so a later first-page polish pass may be useful if the final AAAI page budget allows it.

verification.targeted_red: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k "aaai27_uses_official" failed before implementation because the old AAAI scaffold still used the shared tool-first sections and old style expectation.
verification.targeted_green: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py -k "aaai27_uses_official" passed after implementation: 1 passed, 48 deselected.
verification.full_layout: python -m pytest -q tests/test_paper_layout.py passed: 49 passed in 6.33s.
verification.build: make -C paper aaai27 passed and produced paper/venues/aaai27/build/main.pdf with 9 letter pages.
verification.pdf_text_scan: pdftotext found the new title and Anonymous Submission marker, and did not find the old title, old red-material caption tokens, fig_vlm_grounding_cases, or private local paths.

remaining_items: Run the final AAAI page-limit and supplementary-material policy pass after the target call is locked.
Flatten or package the source according to the official AuthorKit source-upload requirements before submission.
Prepare the final reproducibility checklist and AI-use disclosure once AAAI 2027 submission forms are live.
Run a full page-by-page AAAI visual/readability review, not only the first three rendered pages.

claim_boundary.notes: This pass builds the main PDF and local AAAI story sections. It does not claim the final AAAI upload package is complete.
